name: Anthropic
headline: Anthropic POST-HOLD REASSESSMENT: 60.0 → 58.1 (-1.9); Functional band sustained; BOUNDARY CASE flagged. Composite moves down 1.9pt: the RSP v3 pre-deployment safety-pledge drop (Feb 26) is the heaviest single SYS regression in the AI labs cluster this year and the methodology's first canonical 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' event; partially offset by the March 26 Judge Lin injunction win — first full external-accountability-reversal in the AI labs cluster — and maintained hard red lines on autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance under documented Pentagon contracting pressure. Composite 58.1 sits 2.9pt above the Developing/Functional boundary (40/41) and 2.9pt below the Functional/Established boundary (60/61). The proposed score is NOT a boundary cross, but the published 60.0 was itself sitting at the working Functional/Established pivot, so any movement risks band perception even when within the same band. Confidence MEDIUM (six concurrent evidence streams; first post-hold reassessment after a flagship-commitment reversal of this size; structural divergence between SYS regression and ACC/INT positive signals).

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Anticipatory-awareness infrastructure (alignment team, Transparency Hub) operational across window. Mythos Preview preceded by stated harm-assessment for partner-restricted deployment. No documented A1 detection failure in window.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. No material EMP-dimension events in window.

ACT.rationale: DOWNGRADE -0.1. AC2 proportionality: Mythos Preview limited-partner dual-use deployment without publicly-disclosed safety-evaluation methodology is a half-step proportionality drift from Anthropic's prior published-evaluation-first norm. Moonshot R&D postponement (May 5: May 15 → September 30) is neutral process signal. Combined effect a small ACT regression, not a structural shift.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. No material EQU-dimension events in window.

BND.rationale: Sustained. Boundary infrastructure (AUP, partner agreements, internal governance) operational. Mythos Preview partner-restriction is consistent with B3 scope-clarity expectations.

ACC.rationale: UPGRADE +0.2. March 26 court injunction (Judge Lin) restoring federal agency Claude access after Pentagon blacklist is a FULL EXTERNAL-ACCOUNTABILITY-REVERSAL event — Anthropic successfully invoked court accountability to reverse a government adverse action, with the judge characterizing the Pentagon blacklist as 'Orwellian.' This is the first full-category external-accountability-reversal in the AI labs cluster (contrast xAI May 14 partial-external-accountability-reversal: disclosure under same-day pressure with forward-looking remediation only). AB2 correction-willingness and AB3 transparency both elevated by court-record visibility of the Pentagon-pressure documentation. Half-step upgrade reflects the structural strength of the precedent.

SYS.rationale: DOWNGRADE -0.8. RSP v3 (Feb 26) dropped the pre-deployment safety guarantee pledge — the most-cited Anthropic governance differentiator. Time, CNN, Engadget all characterized this as removing Anthropic's 'flagship safety pledge' under Pentagon contracting pressure. S1 causal reasoning: the policy reversal occurred under documented external commercial pressure (Pentagon contracting). S2 long-term impact: the precedent established is that contracting pressure can move pre-deployment safety commitments at a frontier lab — this is a systems-level negative for the AI safety ecosystem. The -0.8 downgrade (anchor 4 → anchor 3) is methodologically aggressive but proportional to the magnitude of the structural-commitment reversal: the prior 3.9 score was anchored substantially in the published RSP-v2 pre-deployment-pledge commitment, and the removal of that commitment requires a sub-anchor reset. NEW CONDUCT CATEGORY: 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' — this is the canonical instance; methodology should formally adopt the category.

INT.rationale: UPGRADE +0.1. I1 consistency under pressure: Anthropic maintained the two hard red lines (autonomous weapons, mass domestic surveillance) under Pentagon contracting pressure — and PAID a documented commercial cost (May 1 Pentagon classified-AI cohort exclusion confirms the cost is real, not rhetorical). The fact that ONE part of the structural commitment held while another moved (RSP dropped, red lines maintained) is an internally-mixed INT signal: the integrity profile is selective rather than uniform under pressure. Half-step upgrade because the maintained red lines are documented to have actual commercial cost; the RSP-drop offsets within the same dimension (selective integrity is still partially integrity). Note: a more aggressive +0.3 upgrade (e.g. crediting the sector-context observation that xAI gained Pentagon access by accepting no restrictions while Anthropic lost it by maintaining limits) was considered but rejected because the within-dimension RSP-drop offset constrains the net INT upgrade.

evidence.summary: Anthropic published RSP v3 February 26 2026 — dropped the pre-deployment safety guarantee pledge under Pentagon contracting pressure. Time, CNN, Engadget coverage characterized this as removing Anthropic's 'flagship safety pledge.'
Engadget: 'Anthropic weakens its safety pledge in the wake of the Pentagon's pressure campaign.' Confirms causal attribution of the change to government contracting pressure.

Judge Lin granted Anthropic preliminary injunction March 26 restoring federal agency Claude access; characterized Pentagon blacklist as 'Orwellian.' First full-category external-accountability-reversal in the AI labs cluster.
TechRadar: federal workers' Claude access restored after judge ditches Trump/Hegseth Anthropic ban; 'Orwellian notion' characterization.

Anthropic continues to refuse to remove restrictions on lethal autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance; Pentagon May 1 classified-AI cohort exclusion confirms documented commercial cost of these limits.
Mythos Preview April 7 2026 — cybersecurity dual-use deployment to limited partners. CNBC April 10 reported Vance/Bessent White House consultation pre-release and active carve-out drafting.
Anthropic RSP v3 — primary-source documentation of the policy change.
May 5 announcement: Moonshot R&D target shifted May 15 → September 30. Neutral process signal.

keyEvidence: Feb 26 2026: RSP v3 dropped pre-deployment safety guarantee — flagship-commitment reversal under documented Pentagon contracting pressure (Time, CNN, Engadget) — first canonical 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' event.
March 26 2026: Judge Lin court injunction restored federal Claude access; 'Orwellian' Pentagon-blacklist characterization — first full external-accountability-reversal in the AI labs cluster.
Pentagon May 1 classified-AI cohort exclusion: Anthropic alone refused to remove lethal-autonomous-weapons and mass-domestic-surveillance restrictions — documented commercial cost.
April 7 Mythos Preview: dual-use cybersecurity deployment to limited partners without disclosed safety-evaluation methodology.
May 5: Moonshot R&D target postponed from May 15 to September 30 — neutral process signal.
Net dimensions: SYS -0.8, ACT -0.1, ACC +0.2, INT +0.1 → composite 60.0 → 58.1.
rationale: Composite delta -1.9 reflects compound dual-direction evidence resolved into a primary SYS regression (RSP v3 pre-deployment-pledge drop, -0.8) partially offset by ACC upgrade (court win, +0.2) and INT upgrade (red-line maintenance with documented commercial cost, +0.1), plus minor ACT regression (Mythos proportionality drift, -0.1). The SYS -0.8 anchors the assessment: the pre-deployment-safety-guarantee was the most-cited Anthropic governance differentiator and its removal under Pentagon contracting pressure is a flagship-commitment reversal. The court win is real but cannot offset the loss of a published structural commitment — accountability-mechanism activation is reactive, while pre-deployment safety pledges are proactive structural commitments. Math: (3.6+3.4+3.5+3.1+3.4+3.5+3.1+3.0)/8 = 26.6/8 = 3.325 → ((3.325-1)/4)*100 = 58.125 → 58.1. Confidence MEDIUM because: (a) the methodology has not previously processed a safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure event at this magnitude and the SYS -0.8 weight is the assessor's best judgment but is not anchored in prior cycle precedent; (b) the boundary status of the published 60.0 score makes any composite movement perception-sensitive; (c) the compound dual-direction evidence structure (one major negative + multiple partial positives) is unusual at boundary entities.
watches: RSP v3 implementation details — any further structural-commitment changes
Pentagon contracting posture — DC Circuit oral arguments (pending)
Mythos Preview public safety-evaluation methodology disclosure (currently absent)
White House carve-out final form — uniformity vs. carve-out posture is INT-dimension forward signal
September 30 Moonshot R&D delivery — was postponed from May 15
Boundary-band volatility: composite 58.1 sits 2.9pt above 40/41 and 2.9pt below 60/61
carryForward: Boundary-band entity sensitivity to dimensional movement
Pentagon exclusion as INT-positive sector-relative signal
Maintained hard red lines (autonomous weapons, domestic surveillance) under contracting pressure
Court injunction precedent as ACC-positive accountability-mechanism activation
newConductCategoryNote: PROPOSED NEW METHODOLOGY CATEGORY: 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' — Anthropic RSP v3 pre-deployment-pledge drop under Pentagon contracting pressure is the canonical instance. The category should be formally distinguished from 'voluntary-policy-evolution' (no external pressure documented) and from 'external-accountability-induced-reform' (pressure produces positive reform). Should be a SYS-dimension primary anchor with high weight, because flagship structural commitments under government pressure are the canonical floor-of-organizational-integrity test. SECOND CATEGORY OBSERVATION: the AI labs cluster now contains both a FULL external-accountability-reversal (Anthropic court win — completed accountability-mechanism activation with substantive judicial finding) and a PARTIAL external-accountability-reversal (xAI May 14 GitHub disclosure under same-day pressure with forward-looking remediation only). The full/partial distinction is now methodologically established.
boundaryWatchResolution: BOUNDARY CASE flagged. Proposed composite 58.1 is 2.9pt above the Developing/Functional boundary (40/41) and 2.9pt below the Functional/Established boundary (60/61) — within the same Functional band as published 60.0. The published 60.0 sat at the exact working pivot of Functional/Established; the proposed 58.1 moves the entity 1.9pt into the central Functional band, away from the upper boundary. This is a 'boundary-departure' movement rather than a 'boundary-cross' movement. Human reviewer should consider: (a) is the SYS -0.8 weight defensible as the canonical anchor weight for a safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure event; (b) does the ACC +0.2 and INT +0.1 adequately reflect the court-win precedent and red-line-maintenance commercial-cost evidence; (c) does the composite 58.1 fairly represent the structural change in Anthropic's governance profile.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. -1.9 composite delta from SYS -0.8, ACT -0.1, ACC +0.2, INT +0.1 net change. No math drift flag.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
